draft.introduction: A call to worship that transitions into a divine warning against hardness of heart. Hebrews 3–4 builds its entire theology of “entering God’s rest” on this psalm.

translation.text: Oh come, let us sing to the Lord; let us make a joyful noise to the rock of our salvation. For the Lord is a great God, and a great King above all gods. For he is our God, and we are the people of his pasture.

Today, if you hear his voice, do not harden your hearts, as at Meribah, as on the day at Massah in the wilderness. For forty years I loathed that generation. Therefore I swore in my wrath: They shall not enter my rest.
translation.original_text: σήμερον ἐὰν τῆς φωνῆς αὐτοῦ ἀκούσητε μὴ σκληρύνητε τὰς καρδίας ὑμῶν

translator_notes.note: The word σήμερον (“today”) is emphasized in Hebrews 3:13 as indicating the perpetual present tense of God’s invitation—the offer of rest is always available and always urgent.
